items.title: Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak 'for decades' spreads to WA, Qld and SA

children.content: An ongoing diphtheria outbreak in the Northern Territory has now spread across its borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with Health Minister Mark Butler describing it as the nation's worst "for decades".

gallery.altText: Mark Butler wearing a dark suit.

thumbnail.altText: Mark Butler wearing a dark suit.

avatar.alt: A journalist wearing a button-up shirt, with a neutral facial expression.

items.title: Power network's $10 million plan to combat SA's repeated regional blackouts

children.content: SA Power Networks is investing about $10 million to combat insulator pollution, which has been linked to repeated power outages across regional South Australia over summer.

image.altText: Silhouette of transmission lines at dusk with sun flaring through

items.title: Network Ten to go off air in country SA and NSW as WIN Television deal lapses

children.content: The end of a broadcast deal between Network Ten and Win Television is set to leave three regional markets without access to multiple channels.

image.altText: Sign of the number 10 in a blue circle on a brown wall

items.title: Anger as 140-year-old flour mill walls demolished to build hotel despite outcry

children.content: In the face of protests, the remnants of a historic flour mill in Mount Gambier are now a pile of crumbled stone. The site will be developed into a multi-storey hotel complex and townhouses.

gallery.altText: Heavy machinery claws away the stone wall, which is fenced.
Heavy machinery removing walls of stone structure, rubble in the forefront
Orange heavy machinery remove pieces of stone and timber with heritage structure in the background

thumbnail.altText: Heavy machinery claws away the stone wall, which is fenced.
Heavy machinery removing walls of stone structure, rubble in the forefront
Orange heavy machinery remove pieces of stone and timber with heritage structure in the background

items.title: National Trust SA faces inquiry motion amid $230,000 property dispute

children.content: Another National of Trust South Australia branch goes public about its financial dispute with the high-profile heritage group, as the state opposition announces it will refer members' concerns to a parliamentary committee.

image.altText: A middle aged man in a T shirt standing in front of several historical cars

items.title: SA premier plays down social media posts attempting to reveal his home address

children.content: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas has played down social media posts attempting to reveal his home address in the aftermath of hundreds of trees being removed for the $45 million North Adelaide Golf Course.

image.altText: Premier Peter Malinauskas is wearing a suit and standing in front of new homes

items.title: Overseas company makes billions in profit but is receiving millions in government bailouts

children.content: China is "playing a hard game" with its stranglehold on the global minerals and metals market and Australian smelters need taxpayer support to "play hard back".

articles.title: PM 'confident' about Whyalla amid work to bring furnace 'back to life'

children.content: Anthony Albanese says he remains "very confident" in the future of steelmaking in Whyalla, and says the federal government is "continuing to work" with lead producer Nyrstar to secure its operations in Port Pirie and Hobart.

image.altText: A view of the Whyalla steelworks.

articles.title: Steel and lead are facing trial by fire in SA's regional cities

children.content: The South Australian government has put all its chips on the table for the Whyalla steelworks and Port Pirie lead smelter. The next six months will be critical.

image.altText: A worker at the face of the blast furnace at the Whyalla steelworks.

gallery.altText: Two large white buildings at a metal smelter by a port.

thumbnail.altText: Two large white buildings at a metal smelter by a port.

items.title: 'Predator' arsonist's $1.5m fire an 'act of retribution', court told

children.content: The guilty man embarked on a two-week crime spree after his relationship soured with his business partner, who he also assaulted in front of the man's children.

image.altText: old hotel on fire

items.title: Elk have been spotted in the wild in SA. Where did they come from?

children.content: A South Australian landowner has spotted elk on her property, outside containment lines of deer farms. There are no known established wild elk herds in this state.

image.altText: two large elk stand next to bushland.

items.title: 'This is the end': SA health chief recalls terrifying moment she was hit by car

children.content: SA public health chief Nicola Spurrier opens up about the moment when she was knocked from her bike by a car last year, and recounted the experience — which left her with broken bones and fearing for her life — only hours after a 39-year-old motorcyclist was killed in a crash in the Adelaide Hills.

image.altText: A woman pulls the top of her blouse aside, showing a 5-centimetre scar on her left shoulder

items.title: Students of newly merged Adelaide University protest changing of graduation venue

children.content: The newly merged Adelaide University has changed the venue of its graduation ceremonies from Bonython Hall, to Pridham Hall with students launching a petition in opposition.

image.altText: Bonython Hall - an old heritage brick building in the Adelaide CBD

items.title: Stranded driver, students among 10 rescues during heavy rainfall in NSW and Qld

children.content: Almost 50 high school students and teachers have been rescued after becoming stranded by floodwater at a national park in the Scenic Rim while on a school camp.
Earlier, near the New South Wales and Queensland border, crews worked to rescue a person stuck in a vehicle at Numinbah Valley near Murwillumbah.
NSW SES volunteers and emergency service partners have responded to 197 incidents since Monday afternoon.

gallery.altText: A mountain range in regional Queensland.

thumbnail.altText: A mountain range in regional Queensland.

items.title: Tax breaks reward those who can afford to take risks. What about the rest of us?

children.content: As the federal budget prompts howls of protest from those who earn the bulk of their income from owning assets rather than working, we shouldn't forget the relative privilege that has allowed many of those people to take those risks.

image.altText: A graphic showing a number of currency notes

items.title: More jet fuel secured from China, Albanese says

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese announces the federal government has secured 600,000 barrels of jet fuel from China. Follow live.

image.altText: Close up of Anthony Albanese wearing a white hard hat and glasses.

items.title: Mystery illness killing magpies sparks fears of population decline

children.content: Researchers say they are still far from identifying the cause of a deadly paralytic illness spreading through populations of the iconic Australian birds.

gallery.altText: A mapgie pokes its head out of the towel it is wrapped in.

thumbnail.altText: A mapgie pokes its head out of the towel it is wrapped in.

items.title: Spotting these AI relationship warning signs may avoid a delusional spiral

children.content: Intensive chatbot use may be amplifying dangerous and harmful delusions for some users. Here are some of the "red flags" for "AI psychosis".

image.altText: Hands typing on a smartphone with a glowing screen in low-light

items.title: Xi to welcome Putin for visit days after hosting Trump

children.content: While this meeting comes just days after Beijing hosted the US president, the Kremlin insists there is "no correlation" between the visits.

image.altText: Two men shake hands in front of Chinese and Russian flags

items.title: The dead fish waste disaster that Tasmania failed to plan for

children.content: High-volume salmon mortalities last year swamped Tasmania's waste streams as companies scrambled to find somewhere to accept them, documents show. There are now concerns the state remains unprepared for future mass deaths.

gallery.altText: A large amount of fish are laid out in a trench
a skip bin with salmon carcasses

thumbnail.altText: A large amount of fish are laid out in a trench
a skip bin with salmon carcasses

items.title: Shooters kill three people at San Diego mosque in suspected hate crime

children.content: Two teenage gunmen opened fire at San Diego's largest mosque, killing three men before they were later found dead, police say.

image.altText: A police officer holding a gun

items.title: Ebola treatment centres open in Congo as WHO sends team

children.content: The cash-strapped World Health Organization is sending a team of experts to the Democratic Republic of the Congo as hundreds of cases of Ebola are suspected.

image.altText: A man wearing a medical face mask and protective clothing reaches his hand through an open car window.

items.title: 'Nowhere for me to exist': Growing up as a biracial child

children.content: KA was "stripped" of her culture as a child and has been on a journey to reclaim her connection to her Filipino heritage.

gallery.altText: A woman doing a selfie in a bathroom mirror of tattoos on her neck

thumbnail.altText: A woman doing a selfie in a bathroom mirror of tattoos on her neck

items.title: What Kylie Minogue learned from Michael Hutchence and Nick Cave

children.content: Australia's reigning Queen of Pop discusses her relationships with Nick Cave and the late Michael Hutchence, and being tapped to headline the AFL grand final's pre-game entertainment.

image.altText: pop star Kylie Minogue sits in a dressing gown on a deck chair smiling pensively.

items.title: Money stress causing tension in your relationship? This might help

children.content: While financial tension between partners isn't always about money, many people clash over values, spending habits and how they respond to financial pressure.

image.altText: family in kitchen with baby and child

items.title: Road with no name leads emergency crews astray

children.content: When Chris Stehbens spied a lost ambulance driving up and down his road during a Triple Zero (000) emergency response, he was not surprised by the struggle to find the right address.

items.title: Hawthorn 'extremely disappointed' by AFL's call to end Tasmanian partnership

children.content: Hawthorn is less than pleased after being forced to cease playing home games in Tasmania ahead of the Devils' AFL entry.

image.altText: Jai Newcombe holding a football in his right hand

items.title: No long-term rentals but 200 short-stay listings in WA towns

children.content: There are renewed calls for a ban on short-stay rentals as a new report shows holiday homes vastly outnumber permanent rentals in small regional communities.

image.altText: A sign that says Nannup next to a road and greenery.

items.title: 'I wish I were dead': The heartbreaking toll of Australia's favourite pastime

children.content: Australians are the world's biggest gamblers, losing billions every year. But the true cost goes far beyond the financial.

image.altText: A photo of a middle-aged man leaning back on a sofa with his head in his hands.

items.title: ASX jumps more than 1 per cent in rebound rally

children.content: The local share market is rebounding from Monday's heavy selling, as shares in Tuas make back some of their heavy losses. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Trump says he has postponed a planned new strike on Iran

children.content: US President Donald Trump says an attack on Iran that was "scheduled for tomorrow" has been postponed at the request of Gulf state leaders.

image.altText: Donald Trump with hands folded looks to the side

items.title: Police union pushes for US-style night court to free up officers

children.content: The NSW Police Association makes its case for night courts at its biennial conference in Wollongong.

image.altText: Hands clasping at prison cell bars.

items.title: Married at First Sight UK off air after rape allegations

children.content: British broadcaster Channel 4 removes all previous seasons of Married at First Sight after rape allegations come to light.

items.title: Two men arrested over stunt at Punch the monkey's enclosure

children.content: One of the men was detained by a zookeeper and arrested after allegedly climbing a fence and breaking into the enclosure.

image.altText: A baby Japanese macaque next to an Ikea stuffed orangutan toy.

items.title: Boomers star Joe Ingles thrilled to come 'home' in coup NBL signing

children.content: The legendary Boomers and NBA star will return to Australia's NBL for the first time since 2009, signing with Melbourne United for two years.

image.altText: Joe Ingles smiles with a towel around his neck

items.title: Bodies of four missing Italian scuba divers found in underwater cave in Maldives

children.content: The bodies of four missing Italian scuba divers who died last week have been found in an underwater cave in the Maldives.
